differences.title: 1. RENDERING STRATEGY
differences.leftDesc: Renders minimal JS by default, hydrates components on demand.
differences.rightDesc: Offers flexible rendering strategies: Server-Side, Static, Incremental Static.

differences.title: 2. PERFORMANCE FOCUS
differences.leftDesc: Prioritizes tiny JS payloads for faster initial loads.
differences.rightDesc: Aggressively optimizes JS delivery with code splitting.

differences.title: 3. FRAMEWORK AGNOSTIC
differences.leftDesc: Supports multiple UI frameworks (React, Vue, Svelte, etc.) in one project.
differences.rightDesc: Primarily built around React, with strong React features.

differences.title: 4. USE CASE
differences.leftDesc: Ideal for blogs, marketing sites, portfolios where content is key.
differences.rightDesc: Suited for complex applications needing server-side logic and interactivity.

differences.title: 5. API ROUTES
differences.leftDesc: Can use Node.js serverless functions but not core.
differences.rightDesc: Provides integrated API routes for backend functionality.

differences.title: 6. LEARNING CURVE
differences.leftDesc: Simpler to grasp for static content focused websites.
differences.rightDesc: Requires understanding of React and Next.js specific concepts.

differences.title: 7. COMMUNITY & ECOSYSTEM
differences.leftDesc: Active community, but newer than Next.js.
differences.rightDesc: Vast ecosystem, extensive documentation, and large developer base.
